text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 12 January 2019 

Dr Susan  
University College Hospital  
National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery, London WC1N 3BG  

Dear Dr Susan,  

Jonathan Blake, DOB: 22-11-1978, Hospital No: J842156 NHS No. 1045827639  
Flat 3, 27 Brookfield Road, London, N7 0QD 

Summary/ Diagnoses: Focal epilepsy following prior mild traumatic brain injury/marked improvement in seizure control with reduced fatigue from remote working/migraine without aura (stable)/mild anxiety symptoms (self-managed). 

Medication: 
- Lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily.  
- Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily.  
- Propranolol 20 mg twice daily (for migraine prophylaxis).  
- Vitamin D3 1000 IU daily.  

I reviewed Jonathan Blake in clinic today. He attended alone and provided a clear account of his progress since transitioning to a predominantly remote work arrangement in mid-2018. He reports improved sleep regularity and reduced commuting-related fatigue, which he feels has contributed to steadier seizure control and better daytime alertness. He remains fully independent in activities of daily living and has returned to light exercise (brisk walking 20–30 minutes, 4–5 times per week) without adverse effects. Mood is good and anxiety has lessened as work-related stress has become more manageable. There are no new neurological symptoms and no falls. 

Seizures: He has focal epilepsy. Prior to the current improvement he described a fluctuating pattern: he can sometimes go nearly a week without seizures, but when they recur he tends to have several in one day, often between 4 and 8. Since moving to remote working and optimising sleep, he has had no confirmed events and specifically reports: Last seizure on 03-Sep-2017. He keeps a digital diary that shows no events since that date, and his partner has observed no unresponsiveness, automatisms, or post-ictal confusion. There have been no auras, and he denies nocturnal events or tongue-biting. 

Examination: Neurological examination today was normal. Gait normal, Romberg negative, no focal motor deficits, speech fluent, cranial nerves intact, coordination normal. Weight stable; blood pressure 122/76 mmHg; pulse 64 bpm regular. 

Investigations: Most recent serum levels (Dec 2018) showed lamotrigine within therapeutic range; U&Es, LFTs and FBC normal; vitamin D sufficient. No new imaging indicated given stable course and absence of breakthrough events. 

Plan: 
- Continue lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily and levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily; no dose changes given excellent control.  
- Maintain current remote work pattern where feasible as it appears to reduce fatigue and supports seizure stability.  
- Continue migraine prophylaxis with propranolol; review in primary care if frequency changes.  
- Safety: he remains fit to continue current activities; standard seizure precautions reiterated.  
- Follow-up in 9–12 months, sooner if any breakthrough focal events or adverse effects occur.  

Overall, his condition is improving, with sustained seizure freedom since September 2017, better sleep, reduced fatigue, and improved quality of life under the current regimen and work pattern. 

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: “Last seizure on 03-Sep-2017” and describes sustained seizure freedom since September 2017. Clinic date is 12 January 2019. Calculate seizure-free duration: from 03-Sep-2017 to 12-Jan-2019 is 1 year and about 4 months (approximately 16 months). This exceeds the 6-month threshold, so classify as seizure free for a specific number of months.
